content.content:
commit eda14de8a2ea4677d89167e66c836a0b80d03d1d
Author: Fabien Penso <gpg@pen.so>
Date:   Mon Apr 20 17:36:21 2026 +0100

    feat(web): add GitHub Issues and Discussions links with count badges
    
    Replace the generic "Report issue" header link with dedicated Issues and
    Discussions links using GitHub Octicon icons (circle-dot and comment-discussion).
    
    Counts are fetched from the GitHub REST API (unauthenticated, public repo)
    and cached in localStorage for one hour. Badges show the count when available
    and hide gracefully when the API is unreachable.
    
    Entire-Checkpoint: aeb7a5dc2c65

file.content:
Running on:
moltis disconnected
Changelog Issues Discussions
Authentication is disabled. Anyone with network access can control moltis and your computer. Set up authentication
An update is available View release
Installation:
brew install whisper-cpp
Configuration:
Add to your moltis.toml: [voice.stt] provider = "whisper-cli" [voice.stt.whisper_cli] model_path = "~/.moltis/models/ggml-base.en.bin" Download models from Hugging Face.
See sherpa-onnx documentation for installation.

Configuration:
Add to your moltis.toml: [voice.stt] provider = "sherpa-onnx" [voice.stt.sherpa_onnx] model_dir = "~/.moltis/models/sherpa-onnx"
Installation:
pip install piper-tts Or download from GitHub releases.

Download a voice model:
Browse voices at piper-samples and download the .onnx file.

Configuration:
Add to your moltis.toml: [voice.tts] provider = "piper" [voice.tts.piper] model_path = "~/.moltis/models/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx"
Installation:
pip install TTS
Start the server:
tts-server --model_name tts_models/en/ljspeech/tacotron2-DDC Or use Docker:
docker run -p 5002:5002 ghcr.io/coqui-ai/tts
Configuration:
Add to your moltis.toml: [voice.tts] provider = "coqui" [voice.tts.coqui] endpoint = "http://localhost:5002"
Browse available models: coqui-ai/TTS
Requirements: Linux, Python 3.10+, CUDA GPU with 10GB+ VRAM

Setup:
1. Install vLLM: pip install "vllm[audio]"
2. Start the server:
vllm serve mistralai/Voxtral-Mini-3B-2507 \ --tokenizer_mode mistral \ --config_format mistral \ --load_format mistral 3. The model downloads automatically on first run (~3GB)

Configuration:
Add to your moltis.toml: [voice.stt] provider = "voxtral-local" [voice.stt.voxtral_local] endpoint = "http://localhost:8000"
Languages: English, French, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, Norwegian, Danish, Finnish, Arabic
✓ System requirements met
Detected:
⚠ System requirements not met

content.text: Now let me look at how the icons are defined — specifically the SVG masks and the CSS that renders them.
Now let me check how other icons are defined — the `.icon` base class — to see what's needed for mask-based icons to render properly.

content.content: No matches found

content.text: Let me look at the base `.icon` class definition — it's likely in the `base.css` or `components.css`.

content.content: No matches found
No matches found

content.text: The base `.icon` class must be in Tailwind output. Let me check `input.css` for the base icon definition:

The base `.icon` class (line 1843) uses CSS mask-image with `background-color: currentColor` — that's the standard approach. Now let me check what the SVG path issue is. The SVGs use multiple `<path>` elements but the problem could be that the SVGs use fill-rule or the paths have holes that don't render well as masks.

Let me look at a working icon SVG for comparison:

content.text:
The problem is the SVGs are missing `fill-rule="evenodd"`. Without it, the ring/outline paths fill solid instead of having holes, making them appear as filled rectangles. These are GitHub Octicon paths that require evenodd fill-rule.

Let me fix both SVGs:

input.new_string: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 16 16" width="16" height="16"><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M8 9.5a1.5 1.5 0 1 0 0-3 1.5 1.5 0 0 0 0 3Z"/><path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M8 0a8 8 0 1 1 0 16A8 8 0 0 1 8 0ZM1.5 8a6.5 6.5 0 1 0 13 0 6.5 6.5 0 0 0-13 0Z"/></svg>

But the `fill-rule="evenodd"` fix I just made is still correct and important (without it the icons would be solid blobs when used as CSS masks). .session-spinner{display:inline}.chat-session-name{color:var(--muted);font-size:.75rem}.chat-session-rename-input{color:var(--text);background:var(--surface2);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:var(--radius-sm);outline:none;max-width:200px;padding:2px 6px;font-size:.75rem}.chat-session-rename-input:focus{border-color:var(--border-strong)}.chat-session-btn{color:var(--muted);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:var(--radius-sm);cursor:pointer;background:0 0;padding:2px 6px;font-size:.7rem;transition:color .15s,border-color .15s}.chat-session-btn:hover{color:var(--text);border-color:var(--border-strong)}.chat-session-btn-danger{color:#fff;background:var(--error);border-color:var(--error)}.chat-session-btn-danger:hover{color:#fff;opacity:.85}.token-bar{color:var(--muted);text-align:center;border-top:1px solid var(--border);background:var(--surface);padding:2px 12px;font-size:.7rem}.token-bar:empty{display:none}.msg-model-footer{color:var(--muted);text-align:right;margin-top:4px;font-size:.68rem}.cron-status-bar{color:var(--muted);padding:4px 0;font-size:.8rem}.cron-table{border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}.cron-table th{text-align:left;color:var(--muted);text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.04em;border-bottom:1px solid var(--border);padding:6px 10px;font-size:.72rem;font-weight:600}.cron-table td{border-bottom:1px solid var(--border);color:var(--text);vertical-align:middle;padding:8px 10px}.cron-table tbody tr:hover{background:var(--bg-hover)}.cron-toggle{width:34px;height:18px;display:inline-block;position:relative}.cron-toggle input{opacity:0;width:0;height:0}.cron-slider{cursor:pointer;background:var(--surface2);border:1px solid var(--border);border-radius:9999px;transition:background .2s;position:absolute;inset:0}.cron-slider:before{content:"";background:var(--muted);border-radius:50%;width:12px;height:12px;transition:transform .2s,background .2s;position:absolute;bottom:2px;left:2px}.cron-toggle input:checked+.cron-slider{background:var(--accent-subtle);border-color:var(--accent-dim)}.cron-toggle input:checked+.cron-slider:before{background:var(--accent);transform:translate(16px)}.cron-badge{border-radius:9999px;padding:2px 6px;font-size:.7rem;font-weight:500}.cron-badge.ok,.cron-badge.success{background:var(--accent-subtle);color:var(--ok)}.cron-badge.error,.cron-badge.failed{color:var(--error);backgrou

content.text:
The `style.css` was previously minified (single-line) and now it's the full unminified Tailwind output. That's the correct format per CLAUDE.md ("unminified so diffs merge cleanly"). The SVG fixes I made are also good to include — they ensure correctness even if the rendering happened to work after the CSS rebuild.

Let me stage and commit:

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(web): rebuild Tailwind and add fill-rule to GitHub icon SVGs

The style.css was stale (minified single-line from a previous build).
Rebuild produces the correct unminified output with all new utility
classes for the header badges.

Also add fill-rule="evenodd" to the Octicon SVG masks — without it the
compound paths render as solid filled shapes instead of outlined icons
when used as CSS mask-images.
EOF
)"
